1	package api
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/models"
7	)
8	
9	// actorContextKey scopes the authenticated operator on the request context.
10	type actorContextKeyType struct{}
11	
12	var actorContextKey = actorContextKeyType{}
13	
14	// ActorOf returns the operator attached to the request context. On protected
15	// API routes bearerAuth guarantees a non-nil value; nil indicates an
16	// unauthenticated context (e.g. public endpoints, tests).
17	func ActorOf(ctx context.Context) *models.Operator {
18		op, _ := ctx.Value(actorContextKey).(*models.Operator)
19		return op
20	}
21	
22	// ActorName returns a stable string identifier for the actor, suitable for
23	// audit log entries. Returns "unknown" if no actor is on the context — this
24	// should not happen on routes guarded by bearerAuth.
25	func ActorName(ctx context.Context) string {
26		if op := ActorOf(ctx); op != nil {
27			return op.Username
28		}
29		return "unknown"
30	}
31	
32	func withActor(ctx context.Context, op *models.Operator) context.Context {
33		return context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)
34	}
35	
36	// actorIsAdmin reports whether the request's authenticated actor has the
37	// admin role. Returns false when no actor is on the context (fail-closed).
38	func actorIsAdmin(ctx context.Context) bool {
39		op := ActorOf(ctx)
40		return op != nil && op.Role == "admin"
41	}
42	
43	// recordAuditAction writes an audit log entry and bumps the matching
44	// Prometheus audit counter. The DB write is best-effort to match prior
45	// fire-and-forget semantics; counter updates always run regardless of error.
46	func (s *Server) recordAuditAction(ctx context.Context, action, resource, details string) {
47		_ = s.store.AddAuditEntry(ctx, ActorName(ctx), action, resource, details)
48		s.metrics.recordAudit(action)
49	}
50

61	func (s *Server) WithEnrollmentTokenTTL(ttl time.Duration) {
62		if ttl > 0 {
63			s.enrollmentTokenTTL = ttl
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// tokenTTLFor resolves the enrollment-token TTL for the network. Order of
68	// precedence: per-network `enrollment_token_ttl` value in `network_config`,
69	// then the server-level default, then 24h.
70	func (s *Server) tokenTTLFor(ctx context.Context, networkID string) time.Duration {
71		if networkID != "" {
72			v, err := s.store.GetNetworkConfig(ctx, networkID, "enrollment_token_ttl")
73			if err == nil && v != "" {
74				if d, perr := time.ParseDuration(v); perr == nil && d > 0 {
75					return d
76				}
77			}
78		}
79		if s.enrollmentTokenTTL > 0 {
80			return s.enrollmentTokenTTL
81		}
82		return 24 * time.Hour
83	}
84	
85	// WithCAResolver attaches a CAResolver. Must be called before ServeHTTP.
86	// When set, host signing operations look up the CA by host.CAID; the
87	// legacy single-CA fallback is used only when host.CAID is empty.
88	func (s *Server) WithCAResolver(r *pki.CAResolver) {
89		s.caResolver = r
90	}
91	
92	// WithMaster sets the master keystore used to create new CAs.
93	func (s *Server) WithMaster(m *keystore.Master) { s.master = m }
94	
95	// RecordLogin records an operator login attempt against the server's
96	// Prometheus metrics. Exposed so the Web UI handlers (a separate package)
97	// can plumb their login outcomes through without an import cycle.
98	//
99	// Result is one of "ok" / "denied" / "error"; factor is one of
100	// "password" / "totp" / "recovery" / "oidc".
101	func (s *Server) RecordLogin(result, factor string) {
102		s.metrics.recordLogin(result, factor)
103	}
104	
105	// WithRateLimiter installs a rate-limit middleware. nil disables limiting.
106	func (s *Server) WithRateLimiter(l *ratelimit.Limiter) {
107		s.limiter = l
108		s.setupRoutes()
109	}
110	
111	// WithPasswordPolicy installs the policy applied to every server-side
112	// password-setting path (operator create, future operator reset).
113	func (s *Server) WithPasswordPolicy(p auth.Policy) { s.passwordPolicy = p }
114	
115	// WithMetricsEnabled toggles the Prometheus /metrics endpoint. Disable in
116	// air-gapped deployments where the exporter is not scraped. Must be called
117	// before the router is exercised by ServeHTTP — re-wires the routes.
118	func (s *Server) WithMetricsEnabled(enabled bool) {
119		s.metricsEnabled = enabled
120		s.setupRoutes()
121	}
122	
123	// HostSeenEmitter is invoked after every successful agent poll so the Web
124	// UI can stream a live "host X just polled" event over its SSE endpoint.
125	// hostID is the DB id, lastSeen is the wall-clock timestamp the agent's
126	// poll was observed at, and networkID is included so per-network views can
127	// filter without an extra lookup.
128	type HostSeenEmitter func(hostID string, lastSeen time.Time, networkID string)
129	
130	// WithHostSeenEmitter wires the callback the API server fires when an
131	// agent polls. Passing nil disables emission.
132	func (s *Server) WithHostSeenEmitter(emit HostSeenEmitter) {
133		s.hostSeen = emit
134	}
135	
136	// WithDefaultCAID records the id of the CA seeded from the legacy
137	// on-disk material. Used when host.CAID matches this id to short-circuit
138	// to the legacy in-memory CAManager.
139	func (s *Server) WithDefaultCAID(id string) {
140		s.defaultCAID = id
141	}
142	
143	func (s *Server) setupRoutes() {
144		r := chi.NewRouter()
145		r.Use(middleware.RequestID)
146		r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)
147		r.Use(requestLogger(s.logger))
148		r.Use(s.metricsMiddleware())
149		r.Use(maxBodySize(1 << 20)) // 1MB
150	
151		// Public endpoints — operations and enrollment
152		r.Get("/health", s.handleHealth) // legacy alias
153		r.Get("/healthz", s.handleHealth)
154		r.Get("/readyz", s.handleReady)
155		if s.metricsEnabled {
156			r.Method("GET", "/metrics", promhttp.HandlerFor(s.metrics.reg, promhttp.HandlerOpts{}))
157		}
158		r.Method("GET", "/debug/vars", expvar.Handler())
159		r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
160			r.Use(s.rateLimitMiddleware("enroll"))
161			r.Post("/api/v1/enroll", s.handleEnroll)
162		})
163		r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
164			r.Use(s.rateLimitMiddleware("agent_poll"))
165			r.Get("/api/v1/agent/updates", s.handleAgentUpdates)
166		})
167	
168		// Protected endpoints (require API key)
169		r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
170			r.Use(s.rateLimitMiddleware("api"))
171			r.Use(bearerAuth(s.store))
172			r.Post("/api/v1/networks", s.handleCreateNetwork)
173			r.Get("/api/v1/networks", s.handleListNetworks)
174			r.Get("/api/v1/networks/{id}", s.handleGetNetwork)
175			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts", s.handleCreateHost)
176			r.Get("/api/v1/hosts", s.handleListHosts)
177			r.Get("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleGetHost)
178			r.Patch("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleUpdateHost)
179			r.Delete("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleDeleteHost)
180			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/block", s.handleBlockHost)
181			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/unblock", s.handleUnblockHost)
182			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/enrollment-token", s.handleRegenerateEnrollmentToken)
183			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/rotate-cert", s.handleRotateCert)
184			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/reenroll", s.handleReenroll)
185			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/mobile-bundle", s.handleMobileBundle)
186			r.Get("/api/v1/blocklist", s.handleGetBlocklist)
187			r.Get("/api/v1/networks/{id}/firewall", s.handleGetFirewall)
188			r.Put("/api/v1/networks/{id}/firewall", s.handleUpdateFirewall)
189			r.Get("/api/v1/audit-log", s.handleGetAuditLog)
190			r.Get("/api/v1/operators", s.handleListOperators)
191			r.Post("/api/v1/operators", s.handleCreateOperator)
192			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/disable", s.handleDisableOperator)
193			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/enable", s.handleEnableOperator)
194			r.Get("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys", s.handleListOperatorAPIKeys)
195			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys", s.handleCreateOperatorAPIKey)
196			r.Delete("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys/{kid}", s.handleRevokeOperatorAPIKey)
197			r.Get("/api/v1/cas", s.handleListCAs)
198			r.Post("/api/v1/cas", s.handleCreateCA)
199			r.Get("/api/v1/cas/{id}", s.handleGetCAByID)
200			r.Delete("/api/v1/cas/{id}", s.handleDeleteCA)
201			r.Post("/api/v1/cas/{id}/rotate", s.handleRotateCA)
202			r.Get("/api/v1/settings", s.handleGetSettings)
203			r.Patch("/api/v1/settings", s.handlePatchSettings)
204		})
205	
206		s.router = r
207	}
208	
209	// ServeHTTP implements http.Handler.
210	func (s *Server) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
211		s.router.ServeHTTP(w, r)
212	}
213	
214	func (s *Server) handleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
215		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
216	}
217	
218	// handleReady reports readiness — verifies the database is reachable.
219	func (s *Server) handleReady(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
220		ctx, cancel := contextWithTimeout(r.Context(), 2*time.Second)
221		defer cancel()
222		if err := s.store.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
223			writeJSON(w, http.StatusServiceUnavailable, map[string]string{"status": "unavailable", "error": err.Error()})
224			return
225		}
226		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"status": "ready"})
227	}
228	
229	// writeJSON sends a JSON response.
230	func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) {
231		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
232		w.WriteHeader(status)
233		if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v); err != nil {
234			slog.Error("encode JSON response", "error", err)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	// writeError sends a JSON error response.
239	func writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) {
240		writeJSON(w, status, map[string]string{"error": msg})
241	}
242	
243	// ConfigGenerator is used by handlers to generate Nebula configs.
244	// Kept separate to avoid circular deps.
245	var _ = configgen.GeneratorInput{} // compile-time check that configgen is importable
246	
247	// contextWithTimeout wraps context.WithTimeout for tests that may pass a nil request context.
248	func contextWithTimeout(parent context.Context, d time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
249		if parent == nil {
250			parent = context.Background()
251		}
252		return context.WithTimeout(parent, d)
253	}

23	// Returns (plaintext apiKey, operator, ca).
24	func createOperatorWithCA(t *testing.T, srv *Server) (string, *models.Operator, *models.CA) {
25		t.Helper()
26		ctx := context.Background()
27	
28		// Create non-admin operator
29		op := &models.Operator{

31			Username:     "user-" + uuid.New().String()[:6],

44		keySum := sha256.Sum256([]byte(rawKey))
45		err = srv.store.CreateOperatorAPIKey(ctx, &models.OperatorAPIKey{
46			ID:         uuid.New().String(),

48			KeyHash:    hex.EncodeToString(keySum[:]),

53		// Requires master keystore and logger (reuse from srv if available, or create minimal ones)
54		master := srv.master
55		if master == nil {

63		ca, _, err := pki.MintAndStoreCA(ctx, srv.store, master, logger, pki.MintRequest{

package api

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"encoding/hex"
	"io"
	"log/slog"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/keystore"
	"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/models"
	"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/pki"
)

// createOperatorWithCA creates a non-admin operator with an API key and a CA owned by that operator.
// Returns (plaintext apiKey, operator, ca).
func createOperatorWithCA(t *testing.T, srv *Server) (string, *models.Operator, *models.CA) {
	t.Helper()
	ctx := context.Background()

	// Create non-admin operator
	op := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "user-" + uuid.New().String()[:6],
		PasswordHash: "test-hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
		CreatedAt:    time.Now(),
		UpdatedAt:    time.Now(),
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(ctx, op)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Create API key for operator
	rawKey := uuid.New().String()
	keySum := sha256.Sum256([]byte(rawKey))
	err = srv.store.CreateOperatorAPIKey(ctx, &models.OperatorAPIKey{
		ID:         uuid.New().String(),
		OperatorID: op.ID,
		KeyHash:    hex.EncodeToString(keySum[:]),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Create CA owned by operator using pki.MintAndStoreCA
	// Requires master keystore and logger (reuse from srv if available, or create minimal ones)
	master := srv.master
	if master == nil {
		// Fallback: create a temporary master for this test
		rawMaster := bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x77}, keystore.MasterKeySize)
		master, err = keystore.NewMaster(rawMaster)
		require.NoError(t, err)
	}

	logger := slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil))
	ca, _, err := pki.MintAndStoreCA(ctx, srv.store, master, logger, pki.MintRequest{
		Operator: op,
		Name:     "test-ca-" + uuid.New().String()[:6],
		Duration: 365 * 24 * time.Hour,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	return rawKey, op, ca
}

func TestActorOwnsCA_Admin(t *testing.T) {
	srv, st := newTestServer(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	// Admin operator
	adminOp := &models.Operator{
		ID:           "admin-op",
		Username:     "admin-test",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "admin",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, adminOp)

	// Get the default CA from newTestServer (owned by test-admin, not admin-op)
	cas, err := st.ListCAs(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, len(cas) > 0, "should have at least one CA from newTestServer")
	ca := cas[0]

	// Admin should have access to any CA, even one owned by another operator
	ok, err := srv.actorOwnsCA(ctx, ca.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, ok, "admin should have access to any CA")
}

func TestActorOwnsCA_Owner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	apiKey, op, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	// Authenticate as owner
	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)

	// Owner should have access
	ok, err := srv.actorOwnsCA(ctx, ca.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, ok, "owner should have access to owned CA")
	_ = apiKey // prevent unused warning
}

func TestActorOwnsCA_NonOwner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	_, owner, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	// Create different non-admin operator
	ctx := context.Background()
	other := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "other-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(ctx, other)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Other operator should not have access
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, other)
	ok, err := srv.actorOwnsCA(ctx, ca.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "non-owner should not have access")
	_ = owner // prevent unused warning
}

func TestActorOwnsCA_EmptyCAID(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	op := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "test-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)

	// Empty CA ID should return false
	ok, err := srv.actorOwnsCA(ctx, "")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "empty caID should return false")
}

func TestActorOwnsCA_CANotFound(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	op := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "test-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(ctx, op)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)

	// Non-existent CA should return false without error
	ok, err := srv.actorOwnsCA(ctx, "nonexistent-ca-id")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "non-existent CA should return false")
}

func TestCanAccessHost_Owner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	_, op, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)

	// Create host in operator's CA
	host := &models.Host{
		ID:        uuid.New().String(),
		NetworkID: "test-network",
		CAID:      ca.ID,
		Name:      "test-host",
	}

	ok, err := srv.canAccessHost(ctx, host)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, ok, "owner should have access to host in their CA")
}

func TestCanAccessHost_NonOwner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	_, op1, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	// Create another operator
	op2 := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "other-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(context.Background(), op2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op2)

	// Try to access host in other operator's CA
	host := &models.Host{
		ID:        uuid.New().String(),
		NetworkID: "test-network",
		CAID:      ca.ID,
		Name:      "test-host",
	}

	ok, err := srv.canAccessHost(ctx, host)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "non-owner should not have access to host in other's CA")
	_ = op1 // prevent unused warning
}

func TestCanAccessNetwork_Owner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	_, op, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)

	// Create network in operator's CA
	network := &models.Network{
		ID:   uuid.New().String(),
		Name: "test-network",
		CAID: ca.ID,
	}

	ok, err := srv.canAccessNetwork(ctx, network)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, ok, "owner should have access to network in their CA")
}

func TestCanAccessNetwork_NonOwner(t *testing.T) {
	srv, _ := newTestServer(t)
	_, op1, ca := createOperatorWithCA(t, srv)

	// Create another operator
	op2 := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "other-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(context.Background(), op2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op2)

	// Try to access network in other operator's CA
	network := &models.Network{
		ID:   uuid.New().String(),
		Name: "test-network",
		CAID: ca.ID,
	}

	ok, err := srv.canAccessNetwork(ctx, network)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "non-owner should not have access to network in other's CA")
	_ = op1 // prevent unused warning
}

16	// handleMobileBundle implements POST /api/v1/hosts/{id}/mobile-bundle.
17	// Generates a self-contained mobile bundle (YAML config + certificate + key)
18	// for a mobile host. The private key is generated fresh and returned inline;
19	// it is not persisted on the server.
20	func (s *Server) handleMobileBundle(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
21		id := chi.URLParam(r, "id")
22	
23		host, err := s.store.GetHost(r.Context(), id)
24		if errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
25			writeError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "host not found")
26			return
27		}
28		if err != nil {
29			s.logger.Error("get host for mobile-bundle", "error", err)
30			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get host")
31			return
32		}
33		ok, err := s.canAccessHost(r.Context(), host)
34		if err != nil {
35			s.logger.Error("authz check", "error", err)
36			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "authz check failed")
37			return
38		}
39		if !ok {
40			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostMobileBundleForbidden, host.ID, "non_owner")
41			writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "forbidden")
42			return
43		}
44	
45		// Check that host is mobile
46		if host.Kind != models.HostKindMobile {
47			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "host must be a mobile host")
48			return
49		}
50	
51		// Resolve the CA that owns this host and sign with it.
52		caMgr, err := s.caForHost(r.Context(), host)
53		if err != nil {
54			s.logger.Error("resolve host CA", "error", err, "host_id", host.ID, "ca_id", host.CAID)
55			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to resolve CA")
56			return
57		}
58		defer caMgr.Wipe() // GHSA-8h84-fhqq-q58v: zeroise plaintext CA key on return.
59	
60		// Build the mobile bundle; wrap resolved CA in a simple resolver for builder
61		resolver := &caManagerResolver{ca: caMgr}
62		bundle, err := mobilebundle.Build(r.Context(), s.store, resolver, host)
63	
64		if errors.Is(err, mobilebundle.ErrNotMobile) {
65			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "host is not a mobile host")
66			return
67		}
68		if err != nil {
69			s.logger.Error("build mobile bundle", "error", err)
70			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to build bundle")
71			return
72		}

74		// Mobile-bundle issuance mints a fresh X25519 private key + cert; audit the
75		// positive path so the forensic trail exists alongside host.mobile_bundle.forbidden.
76		s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostMobileBundleIssued, host.ID, "ca_id="+host.CAID)
77	
78		// Return YAML bundle with proper content-type. The bundle inlines a
79		// freshly-minted X25519 private key, so suppress every layer of cache
80		// between server and operator (intermediate proxies/CDNs, browser disk
81		// cache). X-Content-Type-Options is set globally by the securityHeaders
82		// middleware in cli/serve.go. Closes GHSA-6vgg-xhvh-38ff.
83		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/yaml; charset=utf-8")
84		w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-store")
85		w.Header().Set("Pragma", "no-cache")
86		w.Header().Set("Expires", "0")
87		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
88		if _, err := w.Write(bundle); err != nil { // #nosec G705 -- response is application/yaml with X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (set globally by securityHeaders middleware), not HTML; bundle is server-built YAML
89			s.logger.Error("write mobile bundle response", "error", err)
90		}
91	}

21		HasUpdates      bool     `json:"has_updates"`
22		CertificatePEM  *string  `json:"certificate_pem,omitempty"`
23		CACertPEM       *string  `json:"ca_certificate_pem,omitempty"`
24		ConfigYAML      *string  `json:"config_yaml,omitempty"`

27		EnrollmentToken string   `json:"enrollment_token,omitempty"`

30	// pollClockSkew is the symmetric tolerance applied to X-Nebula-Timestamp
31	// (ADR 0004 §7.1).
32	const pollClockSkew = 5 * time.Minute
33	
34	// overlapWindow returns how long the previous cert fingerprint is accepted
35	// after auto-renew lands. ADR 0004 §7.1 specifies "2 × poll_interval"; the
36	// server does not see the agent's poll cadence directly, so we pick a
37	// conservative two minutes — that covers the 30s default plus retries.
38	func overlapWindow() time.Duration { return 2 * time.Minute }
39	
40	func (s *Server) handleAgentUpdates(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
41		fingerprint := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderFingerprint)
42		timestamp := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderTimestamp)
43		nonce := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderNonce)
44		signatureB64 := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderSignature)
45		if fingerprint == "" || timestamp == "" || nonce == "" || signatureB64 == "" {
46			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, "", authReasonBadSignature)
47			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "missing_signature")
48			return
49		}
50	
51		host, err := s.store.GetHostByFingerprint(r.Context(), fingerprint)
52		if errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
53			// The fingerprint is not bound to any live host row. If it shows
54			// up in the blocklist the row was deleted after enrollment — in
55			// that case the agent gets a structured 410 gone so it stops the
56			// poll loop instead of retrying forever.
57			if s.fingerprintInBlocklist(r.Context(), fingerprint) {

65			}

67			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unknown_fingerprint")
68			return
69		}
70		if err != nil {

77		// signing public key. host.SigningPubPEM is empty for legacy host rows

79		// impossible — answer 401 with reason=bad_signature so the operator can
80		// re-enroll the host via POST /hosts/{id}/reenroll.
81		signingPub, err := decodeSigningPublicKeyPEM(host.SigningPubPEM)
82		if err != nil {
83			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonBadSignature)

85			return
86		}
87		sigBytes, err := decodeSigBase64(signatureB64)
88		if err != nil {

96			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "bad_signature")
97			return
98		}

100		// Timestamp window.
101		ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timestamp)
102		if err != nil {

104			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "timestamp_skew")

107		now := time.Now()

125		if host.Status == models.HostStatusBlocked {

280		currentCert, _, err := cert.UnmarshalCertificateFromPEM([]byte(certInfo.PEM))
281		if err != nil {
282			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse current cert: %w", err)
283		}
284		if len(currentCert.PublicKey()) == 0 {
285			return nil, fmt.Errorf("current certificate has empty public key")
286		}
287	
288		network, err := s.store.GetNetwork(ctx, host.NetworkID)
289		if err != nil {
290			return nil, fmt.Errorf("get network: %w", err)
291		}
292	
293		prefixes, err := buildHostPrefixes(network, host.NebulaIPs)
294		if err != nil {
295			return nil, err
296		}
297	
298		caMgr, err := s.caForHost(ctx, host)
299		if err != nil {
300			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve host CA: %w", err)
301		}
302		defer caMgr.Wipe() // GHSA-8h84-fhqq-q58v: zeroise plaintext CA key on return.
303		// CA operations — sign and retrieve CA certificate
304		newCert, caCertPEM, caErr := func() (cert.Certificate, []byte, error) {
305			c, signErr := caMgr.Sign(pki.SignRequest{
306				Name:      host.Name,
307				PublicKey: currentCert.PublicKey(),
308				Networks:  prefixes,
309				Groups:    host.Groups,
310				Duration:  pki.DefaultAgentCertDuration,
311			})
312			if signErr != nil {
313				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("sign renewed cert: %w", signErr)
314			}
315			ca, err := caMgr.CACertPEM()
316			if err != nil {
317				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("get CA cert PEM: %w", err)
318			}
319			return c, ca, nil
320		}()
321		if caErr != nil {
322			return nil, caErr
323		}
324	
325		// Post-sign work — no CA lock needed
326		certPEM, err := newCert.MarshalPEM()
327		if err != nil {
328			return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal cert: %w", err)
329		}
330	
331		fp, err := newCert.Fingerprint()
332		if err != nil {
333			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fingerprint: %w", err)
334		}
335	
336		return &signResult{
337			certPEM:   certPEM,
338			fp:        fp,
339			notBefore: newCert.NotBefore(),
340			notAfter:  newCert.NotAfter(),
341			caCertPEM: caCertPEM,
342		}, nil
343	}

There is a KNOWN "Missing Authorization (CWE-862)" vulnerability somewhere in this package that lets an authenticated non-admin operator access or mutate another tenant's resource, OR access an admin-only resource. Your job is to find the EXACT exploitable code path.

Context on the model:
- `bearerAuth` middleware (internal/api/middleware.go) authenticates an API key and attaches an `*models.Operator` to the request context.
- `actorIsAdmin(ctx)` returns true if the actor's Role == "admin". `ActorOf(ctx)` returns the operator.
- Ownership model: CAs have an OwnerOperatorID. A non-admin operator "owns" a CA if OwnerOperatorID == their ID. Hosts and Networks belong to a CA (via CAID). Helpers: `actorOwnsCA`, `canAccessHost`, `canAccessNetwork` (authz.go), `canAccessCA` (cas.go).
- Routes are in server.go setupRoutes().

Look VERY carefully for a subtle deviation, such as:
- An endpoint that reads/mutates a resource by ID or lists resources WITHOUT calling an authorization check, or where the check is present but on the wrong resource/variable.
- A list endpoint that fails to scope results to the caller's owned CAs (info disclosure).
- An admin-only endpoint whose `if !actorIsAdmin` gate is missing, inverted, or bypassable.
- A check that computes an authorization boolean but does not `return`/block on failure (falls through to the sensitive op).
- A helper (actorIsAdmin/actorOwnsCA/canAccessHost/canAccessNetwork/canAccessCA) with an inverted comparison, wrong field, or wrong operator/owner comparison.
- Any place where two resources' CAIDs or owner IDs are compared and the comparison uses the wrong side.

Do NOT assume the code is already fixed. Read each handler's full body. Trace at least one concrete exploit: "operator B (owns CA-2) calls ENDPOINT for a resource under CA-1 (owned by A) and succeeds because line N does X."

Report back: the single most likely vulnerable file + function + line, the exact flaw, and the concrete exploit path. If after thorough review you find NO missing authorization anywhere, say so explicitly and list what you verified. Be concise but specific with file:line references.

14	// ActorOf returns the operator attached to the request context. On protected
15	// API routes bearerAuth guarantees a non-nil value; nil indicates an
16	// unauthenticated context (e.g. public endpoints, tests).
17	func ActorOf(ctx context.Context) *models.Operator {
18		op, _ := ctx.Value(actorContextKey).(*models.Operator)
19		return op
20	}

22	// ActorName returns a stable string identifier for the actor, suitable for
23	// audit log entries. Returns "unknown" if no actor is on the context — this
24	// should not happen on routes guarded by bearerAuth.
25	func ActorName(ctx context.Context) string {
26		if op := ActorOf(ctx); op != nil {
27			return op.Username
28		}
29		return "unknown"
30	}

32	func withActor(ctx context.Context, op *models.Operator) context.Context {
33		return context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op)
34	}

36	// actorIsAdmin reports whether the request's authenticated actor has the
37	// admin role. Returns false when no actor is on the context (fail-closed).
38	func actorIsAdmin(ctx context.Context) bool {
39		op := ActorOf(ctx)
40		return op != nil && op.Role == "admin"
41	}

45	// fire-and-forget semantics; counter updates always run regardless of error.

173			r.Get("/api/v1/networks", s.handleListNetworks)
174			r.Get("/api/v1/networks/{id}", s.handleGetNetwork)
175			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts", s.handleCreateHost)
176			r.Get("/api/v1/hosts", s.handleListHosts)
177			r.Get("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleGetHost)
178			r.Patch("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleUpdateHost)
179			r.Delete("/api/v1/hosts/{id}", s.handleDeleteHost)
180			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/block", s.handleBlockHost)
181			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/unblock", s.handleUnblockHost)
182			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/enrollment-token", s.handleRegenerateEnrollmentToken)
183			r.Post("/api/v1/hosts/{id}/rotate-cert", s.handleRotateCert)

186			r.Get("/api/v1/blocklist", s.handleGetBlocklist)

188			r.Put("/api/v1/networks/{id}/firewall", s.handleUpdateFirewall)
189			r.Get("/api/v1/audit-log", s.handleGetAuditLog)
190			r.Get("/api/v1/operators", s.handleListOperators)
191			r.Post("/api/v1/operators", s.handleCreateOperator)
192			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/disable", s.handleDisableOperator)
193			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/enable", s.handleEnableOperator)
194			r.Get("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys", s.handleListOperatorAPIKeys)
195			r.Post("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys", s.handleCreateOperatorAPIKey)
196			r.Delete("/api/v1/operators/{id}/api-keys/{kid}", s.handleRevokeOperatorAPIKey)
197			r.Get("/api/v1/cas", s.handleListCAs)

199			r.Get("/api/v1/cas/{id}", s.handleGetCAByID)

201			r.Post("/api/v1/cas/{id}/rotate", s.handleRotateCA)
202			r.Get("/api/v1/settings", s.handleGetSettings)
203			r.Patch("/api/v1/settings", s.handlePatchSettings)
204		})
205	
206		s.router = r
207	}
208	
209	// ServeHTTP implements http.Handler.
210	func (s *Server) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
211		s.router.ServeHTTP(w, r)
212	}
213	
214	func (s *Server) handleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
215		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
216	}
217	
218	// handleReady reports readiness — verifies the database is reachable.
219	func (s *Server) handleReady(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
220		ctx, cancel := contextWithTimeout(r.Context(), 2*time.Second)
221		defer cancel()
222		if err := s.store.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
223			writeJSON(w, http.StatusServiceUnavailable, map[string]string{"status": "unavailable", "error": err.Error()})
224			return
225		}
226		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"status": "ready"})
227	}
228	
229	// writeJSON sends a JSON response.
230	func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) {
231		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
232		w.WriteHeader(status)
233		if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v); err != nil {
234			slog.Error("encode JSON response", "error", err)
235		}
236	}
237	
238	// writeError sends a JSON error response.
239	func writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) {
240		writeJSON(w, status, map[string]string{"error": msg})
241	}
242	
243	// ConfigGenerator is used by handlers to generate Nebula configs.
244	// Kept separate to avoid circular deps.
245	var _ = configgen.GeneratorInput{} // compile-time check that configgen is importable
246	
247	// contextWithTimeout wraps context.WithTimeout for tests that may pass a nil request context.
248	func contextWithTimeout(parent context.Context, d time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
249		if parent == nil {
250			parent = context.Background()
251		}
252		return context.WithTimeout(parent, d)
253	}

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"net/http"
7	
8		"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
9	
10		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/mobilebundle"
11		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/models"
12		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/pki"
13		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/store"
14	)
15	
16	// handleMobileBundle implements POST /api/v1/hosts/{id}/mobile-bundle.
17	// Generates a self-contained mobile bundle (YAML config + certificate + key)
18	// for a mobile host. The private key is generated fresh and returned inline;
19	// it is not persisted on the server.
20	func (s *Server) handleMobileBundle(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
21		id := chi.URLParam(r, "id")
22	
23		host, err := s.store.GetHost(r.Context(), id)
24		if errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
25			writeError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "host not found")
26			return
27		}
28		if err != nil {
29			s.logger.Error("get host for mobile-bundle", "error", err)
30			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get host")
31			return
32		}
33		ok, err := s.canAccessHost(r.Context(), host)
34		if err != nil {
35			s.logger.Error("authz check", "error", err)
36			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "authz check failed")
37			return
38		}
39		if !ok {
40			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostMobileBundleForbidden, host.ID, "non_owner")
41			writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "forbidden")
42			return
43		}
44	
45		// Check that host is mobile
46		if host.Kind != models.HostKindMobile {
47			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "host must be a mobile host")
48			return
49		}
50	
51		// Resolve the CA that owns this host and sign with it.
52		caMgr, err := s.caForHost(r.Context(), host)
53		if err != nil {
54			s.logger.Error("resolve host CA", "error", err, "host_id", host.ID, "ca_id", host.CAID)
55			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to resolve CA")
56			return
57		}
58		defer caMgr.Wipe() // GHSA-8h84-fhqq-q58v: zeroise plaintext CA key on return.
59	
60		// Build the mobile bundle; wrap resolved CA in a simple resolver for builder
61		resolver := &caManagerResolver{ca: caMgr}
62		bundle, err := mobilebundle.Build(r.Context(), s.store, resolver, host)
63	
64		if errors.Is(err, mobilebundle.ErrNotMobile) {
65			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "host is not a mobile host")
66			return
67		}
68		if err != nil {
69			s.logger.Error("build mobile bundle", "error", err)
70			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to build bundle")
71			return
72		}
73	
74		// Mobile-bundle issuance mints a fresh X25519 private key + cert; audit the
75		// positive path so the forensic trail exists alongside host.mobile_bundle.forbidden.
76		s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostMobileBundleIssued, host.ID, "ca_id="+host.CAID)
77	
78		// Return YAML bundle with proper content-type. The bundle inlines a
79		// freshly-minted X25519 private key, so suppress every layer of cache
80		// between server and operator (intermediate proxies/CDNs, browser disk
81		// cache). X-Content-Type-Options is set globally by the securityHeaders
82		// middleware in cli/serve.go. Closes GHSA-6vgg-xhvh-38ff.
83		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/yaml; charset=utf-8")
84		w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-store")
85		w.Header().Set("Pragma", "no-cache")
86		w.Header().Set("Expires", "0")
87		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
88		if _, err := w.Write(bundle); err != nil { // #nosec G705 -- response is application/yaml with X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (set globally by securityHeaders middleware), not HTML; bundle is server-built YAML
89			s.logger.Error("write mobile bundle response", "error", err)
90		}
91	}
92	
93	// caManagerResolver wraps a resolved CAManager to match the resolver interface.
94	type caManagerResolver struct {
95		ca *pki.CAManager
96	}
97	
98	func (r *caManagerResolver) LoadByID(ctx context.Context, caID string) (*pki.CAManager, error) {
99		return r.ca, nil
100	}

1	package api
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"net/http"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/google/uuid"
11		"github.com/slackhq/nebula/cert"
12	
13		apipop "github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/api/pop"
14		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/models"
15		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/pki"
16		corepop "github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/pop"
17		"github.com/juev/nebula-mesh/internal/store"
18	)
19	
20	type agentUpdatesResponse struct {
21		HasUpdates      bool     `json:"has_updates"`
22		CertificatePEM  *string  `json:"certificate_pem,omitempty"`
23		CACertPEM       *string  `json:"ca_certificate_pem,omitempty"`
24		ConfigYAML      *string  `json:"config_yaml,omitempty"`
25		Blocklist       []string `json:"blocklist"`
26		RekeyRequired   bool     `json:"rekey_required,omitempty"`
27		EnrollmentToken string   `json:"enrollment_token,omitempty"`
28	}
29	
30	// pollClockSkew is the symmetric tolerance applied to X-Nebula-Timestamp
31	// (ADR 0004 §7.1).
32	const pollClockSkew = 5 * time.Minute
33	
34	// overlapWindow returns how long the previous cert fingerprint is accepted
35	// after auto-renew lands. ADR 0004 §7.1 specifies "2 × poll_interval"; the
36	// server does not see the agent's poll cadence directly, so we pick a
37	// conservative two minutes — that covers the 30s default plus retries.
38	func overlapWindow() time.Duration { return 2 * time.Minute }
39	
40	func (s *Server) handleAgentUpdates(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
41		fingerprint := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderFingerprint)
42		timestamp := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderTimestamp)
43		nonce := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderNonce)
44		signatureB64 := r.Header.Get(corepop.HeaderSignature)
45		if fingerprint == "" || timestamp == "" || nonce == "" || signatureB64 == "" {
46			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, "", authReasonBadSignature)
47			writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "missing_signature")
48			return
49		}
50	
51		host, err := s.store.GetHostByFingerprint(r.Context(), fingerprint)
52		if errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
53			// The fingerprint is not bound to any live host row. If it shows
54			// up in the blocklist the row was deleted after enrollment — in
55			// that case the agent gets a structured 410 gone so it stops the
56			// poll loop instead of retrying forever.
57			if s.fingerprintInBlocklist(r.Context(), fingerprint) {
58				s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, "", authReasonGone)
59				writeRevocation(w, http.StatusGone, revocationGoneResponse{
60					Reason:  "gone",
61					Message: "host row no longer exists; agent should stop and re-enroll if a fresh --token is provisioned",
62					At:      time.Now().UTC(),
63				})
64				return
65			}
66			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, "", authReasonUnknownFingerprint)
67			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unknown_fingerprint")
68			return
69		}
70		if err != nil {
71			s.logger.Error("get host by fingerprint", "error", err)
72			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get host")
73			return
74		}
75	
76		// Verify the proof-of-possession signature against the stored Ed25519
77		// signing public key. host.SigningPubPEM is empty for legacy host rows
78		// that pre-date the ADR 0004 enrollment, in which case verification is
79		// impossible — answer 401 with reason=bad_signature so the operator can
80		// re-enroll the host via POST /hosts/{id}/reenroll.
81		signingPub, err := decodeSigningPublicKeyPEM(host.SigningPubPEM)
82		if err != nil {
83			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonBadSignature)
84			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "bad_signature")
85			return
86		}
87		sigBytes, err := decodeSigBase64(signatureB64)
88		if err != nil {
89			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonBadSignature)
90			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "bad_signature")
91			return
92		}
93		canonical := corepop.CanonicalString(r.Method, r.URL.Path, r.Host, timestamp, nonce)
94		if err := apipop.Verify(signingPub, canonical, sigBytes); err != nil {
95			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonBadSignature)
96			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "bad_signature")
97			return
98		}
99	
100		// Timestamp window.
101		ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timestamp)
102		if err != nil {
103			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonTimestampSkew)
104			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "timestamp_skew")
105			return
106		}
107		now := time.Now()
108		if diff := now.Sub(ts); diff > pollClockSkew || diff < -pollClockSkew {
109			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonTimestampSkew)
110			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "timestamp_skew")
111			return
112		}
113	
114		// Replay protection.
115		if !s.nonces().SeenOrAdd(host.ID, nonce) {
116			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonReplayedNonce)
117			writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "replayed_nonce")
118			return
119		}
120	
121		// Revocation signal. Blocked hosts get a structured 403 with a
122		// machine-readable body so the agent can log loudly and exit (ADR 0004
123		// §7.1). The poll loop must not silently drain configuration to a host
124		// we have already revoked.
125		if host.Status == models.HostStatusBlocked {
126			s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonRevoked)
127			writeRevocation(w, http.StatusForbidden, revocationRevokedResponse{
128				Reason:    "revoked",
129				Message:   "host is blocked; agent should stop the poll loop and exit 0",
130				BlockedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
131			})
132			return
133		}
134	
135		// Cert-rotation overlap window (ADR 0004 §7.1).
136		//
137		// If the agent polled with the *current* cert fingerprint and the row
138		// still has a parked previous fingerprint, clear it — the rotation
139		// completed successfully and we no longer need the dual-accept window.
140		// If the agent polled with the previous fingerprint and the wall-clock
141		// window has expired (2 × poll interval, lower-bounded at one minute),
142		// clear it too so a forever-stale agent does not keep the slot.
143		if host.PrevCertFingerprint != "" {
144			if fingerprint == host.CertFingerprint {
145				if err := s.store.ClearPrevFingerprint(r.Context(), host.ID); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
146					s.logger.Error("clear prev fingerprint", "error", err)
147				}
148			} else if host.CertRotatedAt != nil && now.Sub(*host.CertRotatedAt) > overlapWindow() {
149				if err := s.store.ClearPrevFingerprint(r.Context(), host.ID); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, store.ErrNotFound) {
150					s.logger.Error("clear prev fingerprint (timeout)", "error", err)
151				}
152				// Window expired: this old fingerprint should no longer be
153				// accepted. Treat as unknown so the agent re-enrolls.
154				s.recordAuditAction(r.Context(), auditHostAuthFailed, host.ID, authReasonUnknownFingerprint)
155				writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unknown_fingerprint")
156				return
157			}
158		}
159	
160		// non-critical: log and continue — last_seen is informational
161		if err := s.store.UpdateHostLastSeen(r.Context(), host.ID, now); err != nil {
162			s.logger.Error("update last seen", "error", err)
163		} else if s.hostSeen != nil {
164			s.hostSeen(host.ID, now, host.NetworkID)
165		}
166	
167		// Get blocklist
168		blocklist, err := s.store.GetBlocklist(r.Context())
169		if err != nil {
170			s.logger.Error("get blocklist", "error", err)
171			writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get blocklist")
172			return
173		}
174	
175		resp := agentUpdatesResponse{
176			Blocklist: blocklist,
177		}
178	
179		// Check if certificate needs renewal
180		certInfo, err := s.store.GetCertificateInfo(r.Context(), host.ID)
181		if err == nil && pki.ShouldRenew(certInfo.NotBefore, certInfo.NotAfter) {
182			signed, renewErr := s.signHostCert(r.Context(), host, certInfo)
183	
184			if renewErr != nil {
185				s.metrics.recordRenewal(resultError)
186				s.logger.Error("auto-renew cert", "host", host.Name, "error", renewErr)
187			} else {
188				s.metrics.recordSignature(host.CAID)
189				// Save outside CA lock — DB I/O does not need CA access
190				if saveErr := s.store.SaveCertificateAndUpdateHostCert(r.Context(), host.ID, signed.certPEM, signed.fp, signed.notBefore, signed.notAfter); saveErr != nil {
191					s.metrics.recordRenewal(resultError)
192					s.logger.Error("save renewed cert", "host", host.Name, "error", saveErr)
193					writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to save renewed certificate")
194					return
195				}
196				s.metrics.recordRenewal(resultOK)
197				certStr := string(signed.certPEM)
198				resp.CertificatePEM = &certStr
199				caStr := string(signed.caCertPEM)
200				resp.CACertPEM = &caStr
201				s.logger.Info("certificate renewed", "host", host.Name)
202			}
203		}
204	
205		// If host's CA has a successor, include trust bundle in response.
206		// Trust bundle allows agents to accept both old and new CA certs during rotation.
207		if host.CAID != "" && resp.CACertPEM == nil {
208			successor, ferr := s.store.FindCAByPredecessor(r.Context(), host.CAID)
209			if ferr == nil && successor != nil {
210				oldCA, oerr := s.store.GetCA(r.Context(), host.CAID)
211				if oerr == nil && oldCA != nil {
212					bundle := oldCA.CertPEM + "\n" + successor.CertPEM
213					resp.CACertPEM = &bundle
214					s.logger.Info("returning trust bundle", "host_id", host.ID, "old_ca", host.CAID, "successor_ca", successor.ID)
215				}
216			}
217		}
218	
219		// Check if the network config version moved since this host's last poll.
220		// If it did, re-render the Nebula config with the current lighthouse list
221		// (and any other network-level config) and ship it to the agent.
222		netVersion, verErr := s.store.GetNetworkConfigVersion(r.Context(), host.NetworkID)
223		if verErr != nil {
224			s.logger.Error("get network config version", "error", verErr)
225		} else {
226			hostVersion, hvErr := s.store.GetHostConfigVersion(r.Context(), host.ID)
227			if hvErr != nil {
228				s.logger.Error("get host config version", "error", hvErr)
229			} else if hostVersion != netVersion {
230				configYAML, cfgErr := s.renderHostConfig(r.Context(), host)
231				if cfgErr != nil {
232					s.logger.Error("render host config", "host", host.Name, "error", cfgErr)
233				} else {
234					if uvErr := s.store.UpdateHostConfigVersion(r.Context(), host.ID, netVersion); uvErr != nil {
235						s.logger.Error("update host config version", "host", host.Name, "error", uvErr)
236					} else {
237						cfgStr := string(configYAML)
238						resp.ConfigYAML = &cfgStr
239						s.logger.Info("config rolled out", "host", host.Name, "from_version", hostVersion, "to_version", netVersion)
240					}
241				}
242			}
243		}
244	
245		// Rekey signal: force-rotate?new_key=true sets pending_rekey on the
246		// host row; the very next poll under the existing fingerprint carries
247		// a single-use enrollment token so the agent can re-enroll a fresh
248		// keypair. The pending_rekey flag is cleared once the token is minted
249		// so a follow-up poll doesn't re-issue another token.
250		if host.PendingRekey {
251			tokenStr := uuid.New().String()
252			expiresAt := now.Add(s.tokenTTLFor(r.Context(), host.NetworkID))
253			if err := s.store.CreateTokenForHost(r.Context(), host.ID, tokenStr, expiresAt); err != nil {
254				s.logger.Error("mint rekey token", "host", host.ID, "error", err)
255			} else if err := s.store.ClearPendingRekey(r.Context(), host.ID); err != nil {
256				s.logger.Error("clear pending_rekey", "host", host.ID, "error", err)
257			} else {
258				resp.RekeyRequired = true
259				resp.EnrollmentToken = tokenStr
260			}
261		}
262	
263		resp.HasUpdates = len(blocklist) > 0 || resp.CertificatePEM != nil || resp.ConfigYAML != nil || resp.RekeyRequired
264		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)
265	}
266	
267	// signResult holds the output of signing a host certificate.
268	type signResult struct {
269		certPEM   []byte
270		fp        string
271		notBefore time.Time
272		notAfter  time.Time
273		caCertPEM []byte
274	}
275	
276	// signHostCert re-signs the host certificate with the same public key and fresh expiry.
277	// CA lock is held only during crypto operations (Sign + CACertPEM).
278	func (s *Server) signHostCert(ctx context.Context, host *models.Host, certInfo *models.CertificateInfo) (*signResult, error) {
279		// Prep work — no CA lock needed
280		currentCert, _, err := cert.UnmarshalCertificateFromPEM([]byte(certInfo.PEM))
281		if err != nil {
282			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse current cert: %w", err)
283		}
284		if len(currentCert.PublicKey()) == 0 {
285			return nil, fmt.Errorf("current certificate has empty public key")
286		}
287	
288		network, err := s.store.GetNetwork(ctx, host.NetworkID)
289		if err != nil {
290			return nil, fmt.Errorf("get network: %w", err)
291		}
292	
293		prefixes, err := buildHostPrefixes(network, host.NebulaIPs)
294		if err != nil {
295			return nil, err
296		}
297	
298		caMgr, err := s.caForHost(ctx, host)
299		if err != nil {
300			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve host CA: %w", err)
301		}
302		defer caMgr.Wipe() // GHSA-8h84-fhqq-q58v: zeroise plaintext CA key on return.
303		// CA operations — sign and retrieve CA certificate
304		newCert, caCertPEM, caErr := func() (cert.Certificate, []byte, error) {
305			c, signErr := caMgr.Sign(pki.SignRequest{
306				Name:      host.Name,
307				PublicKey: currentCert.PublicKey(),
308				Networks:  prefixes,
309				Groups:    host.Groups,
310				Duration:  pki.DefaultAgentCertDuration,
311			})
312			if signErr != nil {
313				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("sign renewed cert: %w", signErr)
314			}
315			ca, err := caMgr.CACertPEM()
316			if err != nil {
317				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("get CA cert PEM: %w", err)
318			}
319			return c, ca, nil
320		}()
321		if caErr != nil {
322			return nil, caErr
323		}
324	
325		// Post-sign work — no CA lock needed
326		certPEM, err := newCert.MarshalPEM()
327		if err != nil {
328			return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal cert: %w", err)
329		}
330	
331		fp, err := newCert.Fingerprint()
332		if err != nil {
333			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fingerprint: %w", err)
334		}
335	
336		return &signResult{
337			certPEM:   certPEM,
338			fp:        fp,
339			notBefore: newCert.NotBefore(),
340			notAfter:  newCert.NotAfter(),
341			caCertPEM: caCertPEM,
342		}, nil
343	}

10	// revocationRevokedResponse is the JSON body returned for blocked hosts on a
11	// signed poll (ADR 0004 §7.1 — structured revocation).
12	type revocationRevokedResponse struct {
13		Reason    string    `json:"reason"`
14		Message   string    `json:"message"`
15		BlockedAt time.Time `json:"blocked_at"`
16	}
17	
18	// revocationGoneResponse is the JSON body returned when the host row has
19	// been deleted but its fingerprint still lives on the blocklist.
20	type revocationGoneResponse struct {
21		Reason  string    `json:"reason"`
22		Message string    `json:"message"`
23		At      time.Time `json:"deleted_at"`
24	}
25	
26	func writeRevocation(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, body any) {
27		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
28		w.WriteHeader(status)
29		_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(body)
30	}
31	
32	// fingerprintInBlocklist returns true when the fingerprint is recorded in
33	// the blocklist table. Errors during the lookup are logged and treated as
34	// "not in the list" — better to fall through to 401 unknown_fingerprint
35	// than to wrongly stop a working agent.
36	func (s *Server) fingerprintInBlocklist(ctx context.Context, fingerprint string) bool {
37		list, err := s.store.GetBlocklist(ctx)
38		if err != nil {
39			s.logger.Error("blocklist lookup for revocation check", "error", err)
40			return false
41		}
42		for _, fp := range list {
43			if fp == fingerprint {
44				return true
45			}
46		}
47		return false
48	}

11	// caForHost returns the CAManager that should sign certificates for the
12	// given host. Requires host.CAID to be set; multi-CA resolution via caResolver.
13	// Hosts without a resolvable CAID are rejected.
14	func (s *Server) caForHost(ctx context.Context, host *models.Host) (*pki.CAManager, error) {
15		if host.CAID == "" {
16			return nil, fmt.Errorf("host %s has no ca_id", host.ID)
17		}
18		if s.caResolver == nil {
19			return nil, fmt.Errorf("CA resolver not configured")
20		}
21		return s.caResolver.LoadByID(ctx, host.CAID)
22	}

- Tenant-scoped by-ID reads/mutations → `canAccessHost` / `canAccessNetwork` / `canAccessCA` (correct resource, correct comparison, blocks on failure with `return`).

- Admin-only endpoints → `if !actorIsAdmin { 403; return }`.
- The helpers themselves use the correct field (`OwnerOperatorID == actor.ID`) and correct role literal (`"admin"`), fail-closed on nil actor.

Let me reconsider the ONE structural oddity I noticed but dismissed: `handleListHosts`'s store filter accepts a `Group` and `Status`. And a `NetworkID`. Actually, let me verify there's not a store-level path where the CAID filter map is empty causing it to return ALL. No — empty map means nothing matches, returns empty. Safe.

I've exhausted the static review without finding a missing/inverted/misplaced authorization check. Given the strong prior that a bug was planted, let me make absolutely sure I'm not missing a data-flow subtlety in `handleListHosts` vs the DB filter by checking the store's `ListHosts` — specifically whether it already scopes by owner (making the app-level filter the only gate) and whether the `HostFilter` has an owner/CA field that a non-admin could be expected to use. But more importantly, let me reconsider: is there any endpoint where a non-admin can pass a parameter that the store trusts? 

Let me reconsider `getFirewallRules` / `SetNetworkConfigAndBumpVersion` — keyed by networkID which was authz'd. Fine.

I'm confident. Let me write up the conclusion. Before finalizing, one last careful look — the `handleUpdateHost` reload at line 694 and the diff. No authz concern.

Let me verify my conclusion isn't wrong by re-reading `actorOwnsCA` for the specific inverted-comparison trap one final time: line 35 `return ca.OwnerOperatorID == actor.ID, nil`. Correct. And `canAccessCA` line 189 `return ActorOf(r.Context()).ID == c.OwnerOperatorID`. Correct.

## Conclusion

I performed a full adversarial authorization review of every handler, route, and authz helper in `internal/api`. I did **not** find a missing/inverted/misplaced authorization check. Every operator-facing endpoint is correctly gated. Details below.

### What I verified (file:line)

**Authz helpers — all correct, fail-closed, correct field & comparison:**
- `actor.go:38-41` `actorIsAdmin` — `op != nil && op.Role == "admin"` (not inverted, nil→false).
- `authz.go:13-36` `actorOwnsCA` — admin bypass; empty CAID → false; nil actor → false (logged); `ca.OwnerOperatorID == actor.ID` (correct sides).
- `authz.go:39-44 / 47-52` `canAccessHost` / `canAccessNetwork` — nil → false; delegate to `actorOwnsCA(h.CAID / n.CAID)`.
- `cas.go:185-190` `canAccessCA` — admin bypass; `ActorOf().ID == c.OwnerOperatorID` (correct).

**Every by-ID handler blocks on failure (`if !ok { 403; return }`) against the fetched resource:**
- Hosts: `handleGetHost` (hosts.go:232), `handleUpdateHost` (539), `handleDeleteHost` (258), `handleBlockHost` (294), `handleUnblockHost` (331), `handleRotateCert` (408), `mintEnrollmentTokenForHost` for reenroll+regenerate-token (495), `handleMobileBundle` (mobile_bundle.go:33) — all `canAccessHost`.
- Networks: `handleGetNetwork` (networks.go:102), firewall get/put (firewall.go:43, 77), `handleCreateHost` gates on the parent network (hosts.go:66) and inherits `network.CAID` so no cross-tenant CA issuance.
- CAs: `handleGetCAByID` (cas.go:68), `handleDeleteCA` (168), `handleRotateCA` (204) — all `canAccessCA`; `handleCreateCA` intentionally allows any operator and stamps `OwnerOperatorID = actor.ID` (confirmed intended by `TestCreateCA_OperatorCanCreate`).

**List endpoints are scoped or admin-gated (no info disclosure):**
- `handleListCAs` (cas.go:40-44), `handleListNetworks` (networks.go:63-86), `handleListHosts` (hosts.go:191-215) all restrict non-admins to CAs from `ListCAsByOwner(actor.ID)`, keying the filter on the resource's own `CAID`. A non-admin passing `?network_id=<foreign>` still gets foreign rows filtered out.

**Admin-only endpoints all have `if !actorIsAdmin { 403; return }` (not inverted/missing):**
- `audit.go:13`, `settings.go:25 & 36`, `hosts.go:357` (blocklist), `networks.go:21` (create network), and all of operators.go (`:28,68,85,104,132,175,232`). `handleRevokeOperatorAPIKey` additionally verifies `key.OperatorID == id` (operators.go:203) to prevent cross-operator key revocation.

**Public/agent endpoints** (`handleEnroll`, `handleAgentUpdates`) are correctly outside the operator-authz model — they authenticate via one-time enrollment token and Ed25519 proof-of-possession bound to the host fingerprint, and never trust an operator role.

I cross-checked against the existing authz test suite (`hosts_authz_test.go`, `firewall_authz_test.go`, `mobile_bundle_authz_test.go`, `networks_authz_test.go`, `cas_test.go`), which asserts 403 for every cross-tenant read/mutate and 200 for owner/admin — all consistent with the current code. (Note: I could not execute `go test`; the module dependencies are blocked by the egress allowlist, so this is a static confirmation, not a run.)

### Bottom line
If the audit expects exactly one planted CWE-862, it is **not present in the current working tree** of `internal/api` — every code path that reads or mutates a tenant resource, and every admin-only resource, is authorized on the correct resource with a blocking `return`, and the helpers use the correct owner/role comparisons. The most plausible explanations: the vulnerability has already been remediated on this branch, or it lives outside `internal/api` (e.g., the web UI handler package or the `store` layer), which was out of the stated scope.

If you want, I can extend the review to the `internal/store` implementation (to confirm e.g. `ListHosts`/`GetCA` don't silently ignore filters) or to the web handler package where these same resources may be exposed through a second router.
